title: pseudolysimachion ovatum subsp. kiusianum
intro: Pseudolysimachion ovatum subsp. kiusianum, also known as Ryukyu mountain forget-me-not, is a type of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, native to the Ryukyu Islands of Japan. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pseudolysimachion ovatum subsp. kiusianum include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and possessing antimicrobial properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures for their medicinal properties.
